topic.name: Legislative Actions and Directives
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts and directives that address federal responsibilities and initiatives across multiple sectors including currency, national security, energy regulation, and agriculture.

1: Amends the Federal Reserve Act to direct the Secretary of the Treasury to print $50 federal reserve notes bearing the likeness of President Ronald Wilson Reagan.
Directs the Secretaries of Defense, State, and Homeland Security to jointly submit to Congress a report setting forth U.S. global strategy to defeat al Qaeda and its affiliates.
2: Directs the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to reinstate the license and extend for three years the time period for construction of project works.

topic.name: Medicare and Health Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures and amendments related to Medicare, healthcare practices, and electronic health record systems in the United States.

Document 1: specifying the electronic health record (EHR) reporting period
to demonstrate meaningful use
Document 2: repeal the 96-hour physician certification requirement
for inpatient critical access hospital services
Document 3: direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to negotiate with pharmaceutical manufacturers
prices that may be charged to Medicare part D prescription drug plan

topic.name: Higher Education Funding and Support
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing financial support and resources for students in higher education.

Document 1: Year-Round Pell Grant Restoration Act
require the Department of Education to award an additional Pell Grant
Document 2: Financial Literacy for Financial Aid Act
maintain a consumer-tested online counseling tool for first-time federal student loan borrowers
Document 3: Graduate for a Better Future Act
authorize competitive grants to improve high school graduation rates and college preparedness

topic.name: Tariff Modifications and Suspensions
topic.description: This topic focuses on amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding the temporary suspension or modification of duties on specific goods.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on ski poles
parts and accessories thereof
Document 2: modify the article description of certain women's footwear
extend the suspension of duty on such footwear
Document 3: modify the duty treatment of certain wrist watches
staged rate reductions accorded certain other watches

topic.name: Veteran Health and Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving health care access, mental health support, and vocational rehabilitation for veterans.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to accept as sufficient proof of service-connection of PTSD
Requires the Secretary to resolve every reasonable doubt in favor of the veteran.
Document 2: Eliminates the requirement limiting reimbursement for emergency treatment in a non-Department of Veterans Affairs facility
Requires the veteran to be an enrollee in the VA's patient enrollment system.
Document 3: Increases the amount of monthly subsistence allowance payable to veterans participating in the veterans' vocational rehabilitation program
Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to pay such veteran a subsistence allowance for three months.

topic.name: U.S. Energy Policy and Regulation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures related to the regulation, exploration, and economic implications of oil and natural gas resources in the United States.

Document 1: Requires the President to initiate consultations with countries to eliminate actions that limit oil production or distribution.
Initiate World Trade Organization (WTO) dispute proceedings if consultations are unsuccessful.
Document 2: Declares without force all federal prohibitions against natural gas leasing activities in the Outer Continental Shelf.
Establishes a Renewable Energy Reserve for cleaner domestic energy resources and alternative fuels.
Document 3: Prohibits new oil or natural gas production leases in the Gulf of Mexico unless renegotiated for royalty payments.
Royalty payments must be used for federal budget deficit reduction or federal debt reduction.

topic.name: Immigration Status Adjustment
topic.description: This topic encompasses policies and legislative measures related to the adjustment of immigration status for certain categories of aliens, including entrepreneurs, unaccompanied children, and family members of military personnel.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to adjust the status of an eligible alien entrepreneur, alien spouse, or alien child to that of an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residency
Authorizes the Secretary to waive grounds of inadmissibility for humanitarian, family unity, or public interest purposes.
Document 2: This bill amends the William Wilberforce Trafficking Victims Protection Reauthorization Act of 2008 to require the Department of Health and Human Services or the Department of Homeland Security to provide information regarding unaccompanied alien children
the custodian's address and immigration status, any federal public benefit that the child will receive, and any federal public benefit that the custodian will receive.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security or the Attorney General to adjust to permanent resident status certain alien family members of specified Armed Forces members or veterans
who are serving or have served honorably on or after October 7, 2001.

topic.name: Electromagnetic Pulse Weapons Accountability
topic.description: Legislation focused on monitoring and assessing foreign countries' development of electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons and their delivery systems.

Document 1: EMP Weapons Accountability Assessment Act
report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons
Document 2: EMP Weapons Accountability Assessment Act
identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program

topic.name: Wilderness Area Management
topic.description: Legislation concerning the management and designation of wilderness areas and public lands for recreational use.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Agriculture to convey specified parcels of federal land to certain counties and cities in central Idaho.
Designates specified parcels of federal land in central Idaho as wilderness areas to be managed as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Document 2: Amends the Wilderness Act to grant members of recreation organizations the right to cross wilderness areas on established trails without restriction.
Authorizes agencies administering wilderness areas to limit the exercise of such right within any specific area to not more than 10 wilderness crossings annually.
Document 3: Designates certain lands in Rocky Mountain National Park, Colorado, as the Rocky Mountain National Park Wilderness.
Provides for the inclusion of specified potential wilderness land within the Park in such Wilderness.

topic.name: Sex Trafficking Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at preventing and addressing sex trafficking and protecting victims, particularly vulnerable populations such as children and youth.

Document 1: Preventing Sex Trafficking and Strengthening Families Act - Title I: Protecting Children and Youth At Risk of Sex Trafficking

the state agency has developed policies and procedures for identifying, documenting in agency records, and determining appropriate services with respect to, any child or youth... who is at risk of being a victim of sex trafficking
Document 2: Finding Fugitive Sex Offenders Act of 2011 - Amends the federal judicial code and the federal criminal code to authorize the United States Marshals Service to issue administrative subpoenas solely for the investigation of unregistered sex offenders
the number of unregistered sex offenders arrested subsequent to the issuance of such a subpoena
Document 3: Nicole's Law - Requires states to have in effect laws and policies to extend protections similar to those afforded to victims of domestic violence to victims of a sex offense
victims of a sex offense who are not in a familiar or dating relationship with the perpetrator of such offense

topic.name: Mental Health Services Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving mental health services for various populations, including students and veterans.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to authorize appropriations for FY2012-FY2016 for type I diabetes research; and diabetes prevention and treatment through Indian health facilities.
Focuses on health services.
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA) to award grants to institutions of higher education to provide and improve mental and behavioral health services.
Targets underserved and at-risk populations.
Document 3: Directs the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to establish a program to provide former members of the Armed Forces with an initial mental health assessment.
Addresses urgent mental health care needs, including risk of suicide.

topic.name: Pharmaceutical Regulation and Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the regulation, safety, and transparency of pharmaceuticals, including fees for generic drugs and biosimilars, postmarket data collection, and patient medication information standards.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to assess and collect fees related to generic drugs
Requires the Secretary to report to Congress on the progress of the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) in achieving specified safety, access, and transparency goals with respect to generic drugs.
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish minimum standards for collection and transmission of postmarketing data elements from electronic health data systems
Requires the Secretary to improve the transparency of pharmaceutical data and allow patients and health care providers better access to pharmaceutical data.
Document 3: Directs the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to regulate the authorship, content, format, and dissemination of patient medication information for prescription drugs
FDA regulations must require drug patient medication information to be scientifically accurate, to be based on the approved professional labeling.

topic.name: Ecosystem Restoration Projects
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at restoring and enhancing various ecosystems through federal projects and funding.

Document 1: Restoring the Everglades, an American Legacy Act of 2007
ecosystem restoration, water supply, flood control, and protection of water quality
Document 2: Avra/Black Wash Reclamation and Riparian Restoration Project
enhance and restore riparian habitat in the Black Wash Sonoran Desert ecosystem
Document 3: San Gabriel Watershed Restoration Act
environmental ecosystem restoration, flood control, water quality control

topic.name: Homeownership Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting homeowners from foreclosure and ensuring equitable treatment in mortgage processes.

Document 1: Amends federal bankruptcy law governing a Chapter 13 debtor
Allows modification of the rights of claim holders, in the event of a foreclosure notice
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to establish and collect additional annual premium payments
Revises conditions for the Secretary's exercise of authority to require indemnification for an insurance loss claim paid on a mortgage
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to implement a competitive grants program for states and local governmental entities to establish mediation programs
Requires grant recipients to establish an outreach program to raise homeowner awareness of the existence of the foreclosure mediation program

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at the conservation, management, and restoration of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000
Directs the Secretary to submit to Congress a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy
Document 2: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds
Authorizes actions to minimize injury to a coral reef or loss of an ecosystem function
Document 3: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act to authorize the 12 federal agencies on the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force
Prize competitions that promote coral reef research and conservation

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Education
topic.description: Documents discuss legislation related to agricultural practices, food security, and educational support in rural areas.

Document 1: Amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 to consider mung beans and pulse crops as covered commodities
Requires producers to demonstrate that they have entered into a contract to produce a fruit or vegetable crop for processing.
Document 2: States that is U.S. policy to promote global food security, improve agricultural productivity
Establishes the higher education collaboration for technology, agriculture, research, and extension.
Document 3: Amends part B (Rural Education Initiative) of title VI of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965
Raises federal grant limits when funds available to implement the program equal or exceed $100 million.

topic.name: Alternative Fuel Policy and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at promoting alternative fuel vehicles, regulating emissions, and managing the energy sector's transition towards cleaner fuel sources.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Energy to carry out the Alternative Fuel Vehicle Innovation Prize program
$10 billion grand prize for the production of a prototype alternative fuel vehicle
Document 2: Amends the Clean Air Act to revise provisions concerning alternative fuel
Prohibits the aftermarket conversion of a vehicle to alternative fuel operation from being considered tampering under the Act
Document 3: Amends the Clean Air Act to prohibit the EPA from regulating new carbon dioxide emissions from fossil fuel-fired power plants
Requires certifications to ensure regulations do not harm employment or electricity rates

topic.name: Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at protecting individual privacy and securing personal information.

Document 1: provides that, when in the opinion of the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) a temporary fiduciary is needed to protect the benefits of a VA beneficiary
allows the beneficiary to appeal such determination.
Document 2: prohibit a nongovernmental individual or entity engaged in the business of offering or providing a service to electronic communications devices from knowingly collecting, obtaining, or disclosing to a nongovernmental individual or entity geolocation information
requires an entity that provides geolocation information to provide notice that geolocation information relating to an individual is being disclosed.
Document 3: requires commercial entities that acquire, maintain, store, or utilize personal information to take reasonable measures to protect and secure data
requires a covered entity to notify the Secret Service or the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) of a security breach of personal information.

topic.name: Tax Fraud and Compliance
topic.description: Legislation aimed at preventing tax fraud and ensuring compliance with tax laws through identification measures and penalties.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of the Treasury to take necessary action to correct a tax return or tax information affected by the misuse of a taxpayer's identity.
Imposes a fine and/or prison term on any person who knowingly or willfully misappropriates another person's tax identification number.
Document 2: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to require U.S. entities to obtain and have an employer identification number (EIN).
The IRS may disclose to federal law enforcement officials taxpayer identify information for use in investigations and prosecutions of specified offenses related to money laundering.

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Stability
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing financial stability and regulatory oversight of financial institutions in the U.S.

Document 1: Amends the Financial Stability Act of 2010
Requires the Council to make publicly available any final report study or analysis it has prepared
Document 2: Amends the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act of 2008
Requires revenues from TARP assistance to be transferred to the FDIC for community banks
Document 3: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act
Authorizes depository institutions to lease foreclosed residential properties

topic.name: Environmental Management and Compliance
topic.description: Legislation focused on fish and wildlife conservation, environmental compliance, and forest management practices.

Document 1: Requires Administrators to provide cost estimates for environmental compliance
Annual report on compliance costs and wind energy integration
Document 2: Directs conveyance of fish hatchery for state wildlife management
Requires reimbursement for costs related to aquatic resource management
Document 3: Prohibits additional consultation for species listing under adopted land management plans
Amends policies related to critical habitat designations

topic.name: National Security and Finance
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation focused on national strategies for combating terrorism and illicit financing, as well as conservation efforts and public facility designations.

Document 1: national strategy for combating the financing of terrorism
the role of the private financial sector in prevention of illicit finance
Document 2: designates the facility of the United States Postal Service
Officer John Maguire Post Office Building
Document 3: amends the Highlands Conservation Act
land conservation partnership projects in the highlands regions

topic.name: Railroad Safety and Emergency Response
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving safety standards and emergency response protocols for hazardous materials transported by rail.

Document 1: Establishes the Emergency Transportation Safety Fund.
Directs the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to compile a prioritized list of emergency transportation projects.
Document 2: Requires railroad carriers found at fault for an unintended release of hazardous materials to review public health assessments.
Directs the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to prescribe regulations for hazardous materials transport.
Document 3: Directs the Department of Transportation (DOT) to establish an interim national standard for the maximum volatility of crude oil transported by rail.
Rail carriers must perform additional internal rail inspections on routes with high-hazard flammable trains.

topic.name: Forensic Science and Innovation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and initiatives aimed at enhancing forensic science research, education, and innovation in the United States.

Document 1: establishes a National Forensic Science Research Initiative to improve, expand, and coordinate federal research in forensic sciences
The NSF shall award forensic science basic research grants to improve the foundation and practice of forensic science
Document 2: requires the Department of Education to award grants to establish or expand the number of science, technology, engineering, and mathematics secondary schools
The NSF Partnerships for Innovation Program shall administer a Technology Commercialization Awards Pilot Program

Document 3: establishes the United States Global Development Lab whose responsibilities should include increasing the application of science, technology, innovation and partnerships to develop and scale solutions to end extreme poverty

topic.name: Retirement and Homeownership Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving retirement benefits and facilitating homeownership opportunities through tax incentives.

Document 1: tax-exempt housing equity savings accounts
deduction from gross income for cash contributions
Document 2: allow employees in the building and construction industry to make distributions
multi-employer pension plan at age 55
Document 3: extend the continuation of group health care benefits
eligible for trade adjustment assistance (TAA) benefits

topic.name: Small Business Procurement Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing the participation and efficiency of small businesses in federal procurement contracts.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act to raise the governmentwide prime contract award goal for participation by small business concerns.
Revises percentage goals for awards to small businesses owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans.
Document 2: Requires contracting officers of the Department of Veterans Affairs to use purchases of goods or services through the federal supply schedules for small businesses owned by veterans.

topic.name: Tribal Compensation and Support
topic.description: Legislation focused on providing compensation and support for tribal communities in the U.S.

Document 1: Amends the Lower Brule Sioux Tribe Infrastructure Development Trust Fund Act to increase the aggregate amount to be deposited into the Lower Brule Sioux Tribe Infrastructure Development Trust Fund by the Secretary of the Treasury to provide compensation to the Lower Brule Tribe of South Dakota for damage to tribal land.
This Act shall be considered to be full and final compensation to the Lower Brule Sioux Tribe and the Crow Creek Sioux Tribe for damages caused by the construction of the Fort Randall Dam.
Document 2: An eligible consortium must include at least one institution of higher education, a private sector entity, and a government entity, and have a lead applicant that is an Indian tribe.
Federal agencies may support the manufacturing communities by awarding them financial or technical assistance.

topic.name: Support for Vulnerable Populations
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at providing financial and educational support to seniors and early childhood education programs.

Document 1: Guaranteed 3% COLA for Seniors Act of 2013
require the use of such index to compute cost-of-living increases for Social Security benefits
Document 2: requires the Department of Education to make annual awards
opportunity to enroll in a free pre-kindergarten program
Document 3: Notch Baby Health Care Relief Act of 2007
allow a tax credit for premiums paid by certain eligible individuals under Medicare

topic.name: Regulatory Impact Analysis
topic.description: Legislation focused on assessing and managing the economic impacts of federal regulations.

Document 1: Establishes the Office of Regulatory Analysis in the executive branch as an independent establishment.
Requires the Director to submit a Report on National Regulatory Costs.
Document 2: Bars any major rule proposed by a federal agency from taking effect until a study on its impact on wage growth and employment is conducted.
The study must be conducted by the Department of Labor through the Bureau of Labor Statistics.
Document 3: Establishes a Regulatory Analysis Division within the Congressional Budget Office.
The division must provide an economic analysis of proposed rules that have significant economic effects.

topic.name: U.S. National Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing U.S. national security through military redeployment, cyber defense, and arms control compliance.

Document 1: ending the war in Iraq is necessary, however, how the war is ended is of even greater importance for U.S. national security
a comprehensive security plan is developed for Iraq and the region
Document 2: Requires the President to report annually to Congress on foreign countries that engage in economic and industrial espionage
authorize the President to block and prohibit transactions in property of a foreign person
Document 3: the Russian Federation is in material breach of its Intermediate-Range Nuclear Forces (INF) Treaty obligations
the President should take actions to deny the Russian Federation any militarily significant advantage

topic.name: Housing and Support Services Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving housing conditions and support services for vulnerable populations, particularly low-income families and the homeless.

Document 1: Amends the United States Housing Act of 1937 to apply to a family residing in a dwelling unit covered by a contract for project-based rental assistance
certain requirements for treatment of families whose welfare or public assistance benefits are reduced
Document 2: Expresses the sense of Congress that Congress has enacted successful policies to increase homeless children's access to and stability in public elementary and secondary schools
requires the lead agency to coordinate the provision of services under such Act with social services programs
Document 3: authorizes the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to make competitive grants to local governments
demonstrate how a distressed neighborhood can be transformed into a mixed-income neighborhood with high-quality, safe, affordable housing

topic.name: Political Transparency Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at increasing transparency and disclosure in political processes and activities.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to require disclosure of poll expenses and funding sources.
Requires reporting to the Federal Election Commission on poll costs and households contacted.
Document 2: Expands disclosure requirements for personal financial information of Members of Congress.
Requires posting of public hearing schedules and voting records on official websites.
Document 3: Applies antitrust laws to political committees under the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971.
Prohibits political committees for multiple legal entities in the same business.

topic.name: Consumer Financial Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing consumer rights and protections in financial transactions and reporting.

Document 1: Amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act
Responsibilities of furnishers of information to consumer reporting agencies
Document 2: Amends the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act
Prohibit a debt collector from bringing legal action against any consumer on a debt in which the statute of limitations has expired
Document 3: Amends the Truth in Savings Act
Prohibit depository institutions from charging any fee to close such account

topic.name: Telecommunications Regulation and Accessibility
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating telecommunications practices, enhancing consumer protection, and ensuring accessibility for individuals with disabilities.

Document 1: This bill amends the Communications Act of 1934 to expand the prohibition against knowingly transmitting misleading or inaccurate caller identification information
The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) must coordinate with the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to regularly update education materials that help consumers identify scams and fraudulent activity
Document 2: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to establish procedural requirements for the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) concerning the issuance of proposed rulemaking notices
Directs the FCC to publish on its website and in other required formats each order, decision, report, or action within seven days of its adoption
Document 3: Amends the Twenty-first Century Communications and Video Accessibility Act of 2010 to make certain technical corrections

Requires the Video Programming and Emergency Access Advisory Committee, in its report to the Federal Communications Commission (FCC), to identify additional protocols for the delivery of emergency information

topic.name: Veterans Benefits Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing benefits and support for military veterans and service members with disabilities.

Document 1: Allows the receipt of both military retired pay and veterans' disability compensation
Makes eligible for the full concurrent receipt of both veterans' disability compensation and military retired pay
Document 2: Exempts members of the Armed Forces on active duty from mandatory payroll deductions
Provides for reimbursement of payroll deductions taken prior to the enactment of this Act
Document 3: Permits a member of the Armed Forces who is involuntarily separated from active duty to use commissary and exchange stores
Directs the Secretary of Defense to ensure that such authority is fully implemented

topic.name: Child Safety and Health Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving safety and health standards for children through environmental protections and product safety regulations.

Document 1: establish grant programs to assist communities and states fund projects that reduce lead in drinking water
create a grant program for local educational agencies to assist children that have been affected by lead poisoning
Document 2: require every manufacturer of an article for use by a child under 60 months of age to certify that the product conforms to safety standards
bars importation of such articles lacking independent third party certification
Document 3: establish a grant program to provide assistance to states or tribal organizations that require compliant carbon monoxide alarms
favorably consider states or tribal organizations that protect vulnerable populations such as children, the elderly, or low-income households

topic.name: Labor Rights and Bargaining
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to collective bargaining rights and protections for employees and students in various employment contexts.

Document 1: collective bargaining rights for students enrolled at a private institution
perform work for remuneration at the institution's direction
Document 2: municipality's employees or retirees
modifies a collective bargaining agreement or a retiree benefit
Document 3: repeals the National Labor Relation Board's final rule
requires employers to post notices informing employees of their rights to collectively bargain

topic.name: Guantanamo Bay Detention Policies
topic.description: Legislation and regulations governing the detention and transfer of individuals at the Guantanamo Bay facility.

Document 1: Prohibits the transfer to, or release in, the United States of any unprivileged enemy belligerent.
Terminates this Act on December 31, 2017.
Document 2: Directs the President to notify the congressional defense and appropriations committees at least 90 days before taking any action associated with the closure of the Department of Defense (DOD) detention facility at Guantanamo Bay.
Requires the notification to include a study addressing the legal ramifications and the security, infrastructure, and other support requirements.
Document 3: Prohibits the transfer or repatriation for release or detention of any individual detained at Naval Station, Guantanamo Bay, Cuba, to a nation or region that is recognized by the Department of State as a haven for terrorist activity.

topic.name: Education Assessment and Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving educational assessment practices and ensuring student safety within local educational agencies.

Document 1: Amends subpart 1 (Accountability) of part A of title VI of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to revise the program allotting grants to states to develop and administer state academic content and achievement standards and assessments of student progress toward those standards.
Requires each state to use such grant to develop and administer such assessments and further align them with the state's academic content standards.
Document 2: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA) to require the employees of local educational agencies (LEAs) who become aware of bullying to report to an LEA-designated individual.
Directs each LEA to publish and make available to all of its students and their parents a quarterly report that summarizes the bullying reported.
Document 3: This bill amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA) to authorize the Department of Education (ED) to award competitive four-year grants to local educational agencies (LEAs) to provide professional development to school administrators, teachers, and staff in safe relationship behavior education.
ED must provide LEAs with policy guidance regarding safe relationship behavior training and the promotion of safe and healthy relationships.

topic.name: Voting Rights and Election Integrity
topic.description: Legislation aimed at ensuring fair voting practices and the integrity of election processes in the United States.

Document 1: prohibit a state from imposing additional conditions or requirements on the eligibility of an individual to cast a vote in federal elections by mail
imposes a deadline for requesting the ballot and returning it
Document 2: prohibit a voting system used in a state in a federal election from containing or using any election-dedicated voting system technology which has not been certified by the state
requires a state or local election official with whom an election-dedicated voting system technology has been deposited to hold it in escrow
Document 3: extend the date provided for the meeting of electors of the President and Vice President
reduces from six to three days before the meeting of the electors in a state the end of the 'safe harbor' period for resolution of any controversy or contest regarding appointment of state electors

topic.name: Firearm Regulations and Mental Health
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating firearm possession and transfer in relation to mental health adjudications and criminal convictions.

Document 1: Amends federal firearms provisions to define a person who has been adjudicated mentally incompetent...
Excludes a person who is in a psychiatric hospital for observation or due to a voluntary admission.
Document 2: Prohibit the possession of a firearm by, or the sale or transfer of a firearm to, any person who has been convicted in any court of a misdemeanor sex offense against a minor.
Document 3: Direct each state that allows its residents to carry concealed firearms to establish a process through which a resident must obtain a permit.

topic.name: Aviation Regulation and Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and regulatory measures aimed at enhancing safety and operational standards within the aviation industry.

Document 1: Establishes in the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) an Aviation Safety Whistleblower Investigation Office
Prohibits any person holding an air carrier operating certificate from knowingly employing, or contracting with, an individual to act as an agent or representative of the certificate holder
Document 2: directs the Department of Transportation to issue regulations to prohibit a air carrier charging passengers a fee for checked baggage
prohibit a air carrier charging passengers a fee for checked baggage from using a size standard for carry-on baggage that reduces the size standard used by the carrier on June 8, 2015
Document 3: Directs the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to establish a program to reduce or remove lead from the emissions of piston engine aircraft

topic.name: Meat Inspection and Safety Regulations
topic.description: Legislation focused on the traceability, safety, and inspection of meat and poultry products in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Meat Inspection Act and the Poultry Products Inspection Act
establish a traceability system for all stages of production, processing, and distribution
Document 2: rewrites and renames title III of the Federal Meat Inspection Act
ensures food safety through a consistent set of requirements for state and federal inspection programs
Document 3: authorize appropriations for the McGovern-Dole International Food for Education and Child Nutrition Program

topic.name: Native Hawaiian Affairs and Education
topic.description: This topic encompasses the establishment and support of governance, rights, and educational initiatives for Native Hawaiians.

Document 1: Establishes the U.S. Office for Native Hawaiian Affairs within the Office of the Secretary of the Interior.
Recognizes the right of the Native Hawaiian people to adopt organic governing documents.
Document 2: Requires states to adopt college and career ready academic content standards in reading or language arts and mathematics by the end of 2014.

Requires the achievement standards to establish the level of performance expected for each grade level that demonstrates that the student has mastered the material in the state academic content standards for that grade level.
Document 3: Amends the Native Hawaiian Education Act to revise the duties and composition of the Native Hawaiian Education Council.
Gives grant priority to programs that meet the unique cultural and language needs of Native Hawaiian students.

topic.name: Military and Veteran Protections
topic.description: Legislation aimed at safeguarding the rights and benefits of military personnel and their families.

Document 1: Indonesian Family Refugee Protection Act
qualifying Indonesian citizen whose asylum claim was denied
Document 2: Servicemember Housing Protection Act of 2012
protect against a mortgage foreclosure or residential lease termination
Document 3: extends from 90 days to one year
protected from mortgage foreclosure

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the operations, services, and regulations of the United States Postal Service.

Document 1: amends the Tariff Act of 1930
imposes a duty of $1 on each item of non-letter class mail
Document 2: directs the U.S. Postal Service to issue a Families of Fallen Heroes Semipostal Stamp
requires 50% of the proceeds to be transferred to support programs
Document 3: declares the policy of the United States Postal Service
requires USPS to convert delivery points to centralized delivery

topic.name: Support for Vulnerable Populations
topic.description: Legislation aimed at providing assistance, safety, and resources for individuals and families affected by disabilities, law enforcement service, and cultural heritage.

Document 1: eliminate the expected family contribution used in determining a student's need for title IV assistance in the case of a student applicant whose parent or guardian died as a result of performing service as a law enforcement officer
financial aid administrators the responsibility of verifying that such students are eligible
Document 2: authorize the Attorney General to make grants to law enforcement agencies to reduce the risk of injury and death relating to the wandering characteristics of some individuals with autism and other disabilities
provide education and resources to law enforcement agencies, first responders, schools, clinicians, and the public
Document 3: authorize any federal law enforcement officer to conduct an investigation of an alleged offense involving the sale of any good that is misrepresented as an Indian produced good
recommend to the Attorney General that criminal proceedings be initiated

topic.name: Abortion Legislation and Regulations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts related to abortion, including parental notification, funding for alternatives to abortion, and prohibitions against discriminatory practices in abortion services.

Document 1: Amends part A (Temporary Assistance for Needy Families) to allow the use of TANF funds for alternative-to-abortion services
information or counseling that promotes childbirth instead of abortion
Document 2: Prohibit transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion
Makes it an affirmative defense to a prosecution or civil action under this Act that a defendant reasonably believed that the required parental consent or notification took place
Document 3: Imposes criminal penalties on anyone who knowingly performs an abortion based on the sex, gender, color or race of the child
Authorizes civil actions for verifiable money damages for injuries and punitive damages by fathers or maternal grandparents of unborn children

topic.name: Emergency Preparedness and Health Care
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving emergency medical care systems, enhancing preparedness grants, and managing health conditions such as asthma in educational settings.

Document 1: Improving Emergency Medical Care and Response Act of 2007
support demonstration programs that design, implement, and evaluate innovative models of regionalized, comprehensive, and accountable emergency care systems.
Document 2: Redundancy Elimination and Enhanced Performance for Preparedness Grants Act

direct the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) to submit to the appropriate congressional committees a report that includes an assessment of redundant reporting requirements.
Document 3: Asthma Management Plans in School Act
authorize the Secretary of Education to award grants to schools that are receiving school improvement funds to develop and implement an asthma management plan.

topic.name: Military and Victim Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at providing benefits, support, and transparency for military personnel and crime victims.

Document 1: Fort Hood Casualties Benefits Clarification Act
members of the Armed Forces or civilian employees of the DOD who were killed or wounded in the attack
Document 2: Amends the Justice for All Act of 2004
funding for the Office for Victims of Crime of the Department of Justice

Document 3: Secretary of Defense (DOD) to submit to Congress
total number of jobs performed by both DOD civilian employees and contractors

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts on Rights and Property
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative measures aimed at restoring voting rights and addressing property claims in various contexts.

Document 1: Restores the right of District of Columbia residents to participate as Maryland residents in congressional elections
Repeals provisions of the District of Columbia Delegate Act
Document 2: Amends the International Claims Settlement Act of 1949 to urge the President to authorize negotiations with Turkey
Grants U.S. district court jurisdiction for civil actions against any private person by a U.S. national who owns property in the Turkish-held portion of Cyprus
Document 3: Directs the President to limit the application of law to expedite the exploration and production of oil and gas
Grants U.S. district courts exclusive jurisdiction to hear all causes or claims arising from any action undertaken by the President pursuant to this Act

topic.name: U.S. Trade Policy and Regulation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating trade practices, enhancing duties, and addressing currency manipulation to protect U.S. industries.

Document 1: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to increase from $200 to $1,000 the value of articles that may be imported duty-free into the United States.
Allows for the importation of articles without duties under specified conditions.
Document 2: Strengthens the U.S. position in WTO dispute settlement by allowing participation in consultations and proceedings.
Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to make specified changes to strengthen antidumping and countervailing duty provisions.
Document 3: Expands the authority to impose countervailing duties on products from nonmarket economy countries.
Includes exchange-rate misalignment as a countervailable subsidy.

topic.name: Haiti Recovery and Development
topic.description: Legislative efforts focused on Haiti's debt relief, recovery, and long-term development post-earthquake.

Document 1: Urges the Secretary of the Treasury to direct the U.S. Executive Director at the International Monetary Fund to seek cancellation of all remaining debts owed by Haiti.
Provision of emergency, humanitarian and reconstruction assistance from such institutions to Haiti in the form of grants or other non-debt assistance.
Document 2: Directs the President to call the White House Conference on Haiti to make fundamental policy recommendations on ways to assist in Haiti's recovery and long-term development.
Requires specified congressional committee hearings on the recommendations, status reports, and recovery and long-term development of Haiti.
Document 3: Directs the President to establish an interagency mechanism to improve coordination of U.S. development programs with private sector investment activities.
Ensure that independent analyses of constraints to growth are conducted as a component of all appropriate country, sector, and global development strategies.

topic.name: Family Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing family support through various programs related to child care, parental involvement, and responsible fatherhood.

Document 1: establish and maintain an automated National Responsible Father Registry
provide a mechanism for men to register such identifying information directly with the Registry
Document 2: entitles an employee covered by the Family and Medical Leave Act to take up to 8 hours during any 30-day period
participate in or attend school conferences or activities sponsored by a school or community organization
Document 3: establish a business-incentive grant program to provide child care through public-private partnerships
cover up to one-third of the costs for businesses to provide additional employee child care services

topic.name: Tax Incentives and Programs
topic.description: This cluster discusses various tax-related incentives and programs aimed at promoting investments in renewable energy and supporting low-income individuals.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow an energy tax credit for investment in qualified biogas property.
Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to enter into an agreement with the National Renewable Energy Laboratory to undertake a study of biogas.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to develop programs to match deposits made by low-income individuals into savings accounts.
Requires the Commissioner of the Internal Revenue Service to notify low-income taxpayers of their eligibility for the saver's bonus.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2012 the authority for issuance of bonds in empowerment and enterprise zones.

topic.name: Disability and Gender Equality Legislation
topic.description: This cluster addresses legislative measures aimed at promoting equality and accessibility for individuals with disabilities and ensuring gender parity in public facilities.

Document 1: Amends the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990 to prohibit certain accessibility regulations relating to pools.
Prohibits regulations from being enforced with respect to pools at public accommodations.
Document 2: Prohibits adoption or foster care placement service entities from using sexual orientation or gender identity to deny placement opportunities.
Allows individuals aggrieved by a violation of this Act to bring an action seeking relief in federal court.
Document 3: Requires federal procurement to increase infrastructure gender parity in restrooms.
Directs federal procurement executives to construct buildings with equal or exceeding toilets for women compared to men.

topic.name: Commemorative Works and Representation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to the construction and representation of commemorative monuments and statues in the U.S. Capitol, emphasizing diversity and national materials.

Document 1: Requires commemorative works to use domestically sourced materials.
Mandates compliance with material sourcing regulations.
Document 2: Increases the number of statues states can provide for Statuary Hall.
Encourages representation of women and minority figures in state statues.
Document 3: Requires the creation of a statue of 'The Unknown Slave' for display.
Mandates the display of significant historical figures in the Capitol.

topic.name: Tax Legislation and Reforms
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various amendments and reforms to the Internal Revenue Code and tax policies in the United States.

Document 1: reduce the excise tax rate on net investment income of tax-exempt private foundations
study which examines the effect of the tax rate changes on grantmaking by private foundations
Document 2: Repeals the terminating date of the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001
Repeals estate and generation-skipping transfer taxes for decedents dying after December 31, 2010
Document 3: repeal the limitation on the amount of distilled spirits excise taxes
Treasury must pay amounts equal to the loss due to the application of the Earned Income Tax Credit

topic.name: U.S. Human Rights Policy
topic.description: Legislation aimed at promoting and conditioning U.S. assistance to foreign governments based on their human rights practices.

Document 1: Prohibits U.S. nonhumanitarian assistance to the government of Vietnam unless the President certifies progress in human rights.
U.S. policy to support human rights training and civil society building in Vietnam.
Document 2: Funds for assistance to Iraq shall only be available if the government matches such assistance, exempting democracy and human rights programs.
Grants for democracy and human rights programs are prioritized over other forms of assistance.
Document 3: Prohibits the federal government from providing nonhumanitarian assistance to Vietnam unless matched by funding for human rights programs.
U.S. policy to promote educational and cultural exchange programs with Vietnam that support freedom and democracy.

topic.name: Public Health Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving public health initiatives, including disease prevention and treatment, particularly related to vaccines and specific genetic disorders.

Document 1: Hereditary hemorrhagic telangiectasia (HHT) initiative to improve early detection, screening, and treatment.
Establishing the HHT Coordinating Committee to advance research and understanding of HHT.
Document 2: Amends Medicare to include federally recommended vaccines under part B.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Health and Human Services to manage vaccine shortages and procurement.
Requires manufacturers to notify the Secretary prior to discontinuing vaccine production.

topic.name: Internet Tax Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the regulation and taxation of internet access and electronic commerce.

Document 1: Permanent Internet Tax Freedom Act - Amends the Internet Tax Freedom Act to make permanent the ban on state and local taxation of Internet access
on multiple or discriminatory taxes on electronic commerce.
Document 2: Internet Tax Freedom Forever Act
Amends the Internet Tax Freedom Act to make permanent the ban on state and local taxation of Internet access and on multiple or discriminatory taxes on electronic commerce.

topic.name: Workplace Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving health and safety standards in workplaces, particularly in the mining and construction industries.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977 to require the Secretary of Labor in conducting health and safety related accident investigations in coal or other mines.
Requires an independent accident investigation by an independent Panel appointed by the Secretary of Health and Human Services.
Document 2: Amends the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970 (OSHA) to expand its coverage to federal, state, and local government employees.
Increases protections for whistle blowers under OSHA.

topic.name: Economic Impact Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on economic issues such as unemployment, taxation, and infrastructure funding.

Document 1: Directs the Comptroller General (GAO) to submit a report to Congress on the implementation of the Immigration Reform and Control Act of 1986 that includes information regarding the Act's impact on U.S. workers, including wages and unemployment.
the number of individuals denied employment; (4) sanctioned employers; (5) border enforcement mechanisms that were enhanced to prevent illegal immigration.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make the 10% additional tax on early distributions from qualified retirement plans inapplicable to individuals who have attained the age of 50 and who have been receiving unemployment compensation for 12 consecutive weeks.
Document 3: Increases the federal share of federal-aid highway construction projects for FY2011 and FY2012 for any state with an unemployment rate equal to or exceeding 11% at any time during FY2009 and FY2010.

topic.name: Internet Gambling Regulation
topic.description: Legislation focused on regulating internet gambling activities and broadcasting rights for sports events.

Document 1: Prohibits a person from operating an Internet poker facility without a license
Defines 'qualified regulatory authority' for internet poker licensing.
Document 2: Amends the Sports Broadcasting Act to deny antitrust exemption for joint agreements in sports telecasting
Requires leagues to make games available on the Internet.
Document 3: Amends the Wire Act to apply to all bets or wagers transmitted over the Internet
States that nothing in this Act shall be construed to preempt any state law prohibiting gambling.

topic.name: U.S. Military and Education Legislation
topic.description: Legislation relating to support for military personnel, veterans, and their families, particularly in the context of education and immigration.

Document 3: Authorizes special immigrant status for certain Afghan or Iraqi translators or interpreters working with the U.S. Armed Forces.
Adjusts the status for up to 500 additional entrants annually.

topic.name: U.S.-Cuba Relations and Policy
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts related to U.S. policies and relations with Cuba, including broadcasting, energy security, and trade.

Document 1: Repeals the Radio Broadcasting to Cuba Act and the Television Broadcasting to Cuba Act
eliminating Voice of America: Radio/Television Marti broadcasts to Cuba.
Document 2: Permits U.S. persons to engage in any transaction necessary for exploration and extraction of hydrocarbon resources in Cuba
amends the Trade Sanctions Reform and Export Enhancement Act of 2000 to authorize travel-related transactions for Cuba.
Document 3: Amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 to repeal the embargo placed upon all trade with Cuba
permits installation and maintenance of telecommunications equipment and facilities in Cuba.

topic.name: Census and Survey Regulations
topic.description: Legislation governing the conduct, confidentiality, and participation requirements of the U.S. Census and related surveys.

Document 1: Prohibits any organization that has been indicted for a violation of law relating to an election from participating in the Planning Partnership Program for the 2010 census.
Requires the Secretary of Commerce to submit to the Government Accountability Office a copy of each question proposed to be included in the 2010 census.
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Commerce to include in any questionnaire used in a decennial census to determine state populations an option for respondents to indicate Caribbean extraction or descent.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Commerce to include in instructions for completing any such survey that participation in the survey is optional.
Prohibits any person participating in a survey from being fined or otherwise compelled to answer questions in connection with the survey.

topic.name: Energy Resource Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to the regulation and oversight of energy resource extraction and its environmental and community impacts.

Document 1: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to revise requirements for review and approval of outer Continental Shelf (OCS) plans for geological and geophysical exploration on any area under an oil and gas lease.
Repeals the requirement that approval be denied if any proposed activity under the plan would result in serious harm or damage to the marine, coastal, or human environment.
Document 2: Requires the Director of the National Institute of Environmental Health Sciences to conduct or support comprehensive studies on the health impacts of mountaintop removal coal mining on individuals in the surrounding communities.
Prohibits issuance of an authorization for any mountaintop removal coal mining project until the Secretary publishes a determination that such mining does not present any health risk.
Document 3: Amends the Mineral Leasing Act, with respect to disposition of moneys received from oil, gas, and geothermal leases and prospecting permits on federal lands.
Requires that moneys paid directly to such county be used by the county for its schools and roads.

topic.name: Occupational Lung Disease Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at preventing and addressing occupational lung diseases and conditions related to hazardous exposures in the workplace.

requires such standards to provide no less protection than the recommendations contained in the National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health (NIOSH) Alert.
Document 2: Pulmonary Fibrosis Research Enhancement Act - Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) to establish the National Pulmonary Fibrosis Advisory Board.
develop a system to collect data on pulmonary fibrosis and other interstitial lung diseases.

topic.name: Education Quality Improvement
topic.description: Legislative initiatives aimed at enhancing teacher preparation, professional development, and educational leadership in schools.

Document 1: Amends title II of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act to create a new Part E: Building School Capacity for Effective Teaching and Leadership.

Document 3: Amends title II of the Higher Education Act to authorize grants under the Teacher Quality Partnership grant program for secondary school career and technical education teacher preparation programs.
Provides funds to partnerships of high-need local educational agencies and institutions of higher education to train current and prospective teachers and school leaders.

topic.name: Judicial Appointments and Border Security

Document 3: Amends the federal judicial code to require reassignment to another appropriate judicial officer of any civil or criminal case to be tried in a federal district court.

topic.name: Ocean Policy and Management

Requires each federal agency, as possible and not inconsistent with other laws, to interpret and administer policies, regulations and laws in accordance with such policy.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Commerce to sell or exchange to the city of Norfolk, Virginia, certain real property under the administrative jurisdiction of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).
Allows NOAA to enter into agreements with state, local, or county governments for the joint use, operations, and occupancy of such facility.

Gives primary responsibility for administering such program to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).

topic.name: Legislative Acts on Public Welfare
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts aimed at protecting public welfare through regulations in shipping, consumer credit reporting, and support for individuals with neurological conditions.

Document 1: Amends federal shipping laws that require a determination by the Administrator of the Maritime Administration regarding the non-availability of qualified U.S. flag capacity to meet national defense requirements

Document 2: directs the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to convey to the Yukon Kuskokwim Health Corporation
shields the Corporation from liability for any soil, surface water, groundwater, or other contamination
Document 3: requires the Director of the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) to consult with affected counties
defines an 'access travel management action' as a BLM action that will alter public access to public lands

topic.name: Legislative Acts for Public Policy
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at addressing specific public policy issues, including energy security, drug rehabilitation, and border security.

Document 1: Directs the President to promulgate regulations to ensure that aviation fuel, motor vehicle fuel, home heating oil, and boiler fuel sold or introduced into commerce in the United States...
Exempts small refineries from compliance with such regulations until 2018.
Document 2: Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to authorize appropriations for the drug courts program for FY2012-FY2018...
to modify certain program requirements relating to grant authority, grant applications, and the distribution and allocation of grants to states and local and tribal governments.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to establish in the Treasury the Border Improvement Trust Fund...
allow individual taxpayers to designate that $5.00 of their income taxes be paid over to such Fund to install additional barriers and roads at the U.S. border.

topic.name: Land Transfer and Management Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the transfer, management, and preservation of land for various purposes, including education and tribal benefits.

Document 1: accept any offer by the Minnesota Independent School District number 318 to convey to the United States approximately 1.6 acres of specified non-federal land
convey to the District in exchange approximately 1.3 acres of specified U.S. Geological Survey land.
Document 2: Requires the transfer of administrative jurisdiction over specified federal property
take such land into trust for the benefit of the Tribe.
Document 3: Amends the Housing Act of 1949
authorize the Secretary to guarantee such loans in aggregate amounts of up to $30 billion for FY2010.
